sourceMovie.title: E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial (1982) stuck because it commits to its science fiction premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Steven Spielberg's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The science fiction and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Home
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Home (2015) is the natural next step after E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial: same science-fiction DNA, different perspective. The funny streak and family elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: James and the Giant Peach
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial who respond to its adventure will find James and the Giant Peach (1996) immediately familiar. Both films share a family sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Flight of the Navigator
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial fans keep landing on Flight of the Navigator (1986) — the science-fiction is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: Explorers
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Explorers draws from the same science-fiction well as E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial, channeling adventure in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 1985 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: Guardians of the Galaxy
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Guardians of the Galaxy (2014) captures the science-fiction appeal of E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial without simply imitating it. The adventure streak running through both films is what puts Guardians of the Galaxy at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: The Martian
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The science-fiction appeal of E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial — that tense edge, the adventure — translates cleanly into The Martian (2015). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: Avatar: The Way of Water
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind Avatar: The Way of Water (2022) clearly understood what makes science-fiction work — the same quality that keeps E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial rewatchable. Expect the same tense rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: The Iron Giant
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Iron Giant (1999) is the kind of film E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The science-fiction overlap is real, but it's the uplifting emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: Hocus Pocus
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Hocus Pocus shares E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial's gift for family — the 1993 film delivers fantasy without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: Lilo & Stitch
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial compelling — the science-fiction, the family — shows up in Lilo & Stitch (2025) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that funny storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

faq.question: What should I watch after E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial?
faq.answer: Try Home, James and the Giant Peach, Flight of the Navigator—each shares DNA with E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial?
faq.answer: E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial (1982) is best described as Science Fiction, Adventure, Family, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this science fiction lane.
